memory:memory/2026-07-02.md:1:36.snippet: # 2026-07-02 — Lobster Automation v0.2 Shipped + Tunnel Troubleshooting ## v0.2 — Robustness Release (commit `06d6c48`, tag `v0.2`) All 3 modules implemented, tested, and pushed to GitHub: ### Module 1: Smart Retry & Error Classification - `retry-engine.ts` — `classifyError()` categorises errors into `transient`, `fatal`, `rate_limited` - HTTP 429 respects `Retry-After` header - HTTP 400–499 (except 429) → immediate fatal - HTTP 5xx / network errors → exponential backoff - 11/11 tests pass ### Module 2: State-Based Selective Replay - `POST /runs/:id/replay` — finds last successful step, clears child records, rebuilds state, resumes - Returns `202 Accepted` with replay metadata - Tested w

memory:memory/2026-07-04.md:1:32.snippet: # 2026-07-04 ## lobster-automation relay project (PHP relay on ewizt.com) Goal: replace ngrok with a self-hosted relay queue on ewizt.com, so WSL doesn't need ngrok. ### Architecture - External callers POST to ewizt.com webhook endpoint - ewizt.com queues webhook payload in SQLite - WSL lobster server long-polls ewizt.com for queued webhooks (near-instant, no polling delay) - WSL processes and posts result back; caller checks result via GET ### PHP files written (in ~/works/lobster-automation/relay-php/) - `shared.php` — SQLite queue helper (DB at `queue.sqlite`, TOKEN=`NsufkdwqJWfr4GwFko7R`) - `webhook.php` — POST `/relay/webhook/:name` — receives+queues webhooks, returns run_id - `poll

memory:memory/2026-07-05.md:138:172.snippet: - Bandwidth: ~10.2 KB/hr idle (vs 20.5 KB/hr with 55s — 50% reduction) - Poll cycles: ~33/hr (vs ~65/hr with 55s) ### PHP Debugging on ewizt.com - `check.php` — test `max_execution_time`, `set_time_limit(0)`, `default_socket_timeout` - `debug-poll.php` — standalone 110s loop test (confirmed LiteSpeed doesn't kill it mid-sleep) - LiteSpeed kills PHP when the **connection itself times out** (not PHP execution) - The 500 error at 120s was LiteSpeed's connection timeout, not PHP's fault ### Architecture Summary - WSL initiates ALL connections outbound — no inbound ports needed, works through NAT/CGNAT - Long-poll: WSL holds connection open for 110s, ewizt PHP holds until webhook or timeout - S

memory:memory/2026-07-05.md:1:31.snippet: # 2026-07-05 ## ewizt Relay — Long-Poll Optimized ### LiteSpeed Connection Timeout Discovery - ewizt.com runs LiteSpeed (not Apache/nginx) - LiteSpeed has a hard **Connection Timeout of ~120s** — kills requests regardless of PHP `set_time_limit` - `set_time_limit(0)` works in PHP, but LiteSpeed still cuts at 120s - `sleep(120)` works fine (LiteSpeed doesn't timeout during sleep) - Initial poll was 55s — conservative guess. Could push to 290s PHP, but LiteSpeed kills at 120s ### Final Settings - **poll.php**: `set_time_limit(0)` + `$max_wait = 110` (safely under 120s LiteSpeed limit) - **WSL relay-poller**: fetch timeout = 130s (110s + 20s buffer) - Bandwidth: ~10.2 KB/hr idle (vs 20.5 KB/

memory:memory/2026-07-02.md:115:137.snippet: - CNAME at mschosting currently pointing to: `lobster-d68639f4.loca.lt` (not working due to Host header) ### Fundamental Blocker All tunnel services (localtunnel, cloudflared, serveo, localhost.run) validate the Host header and only accept their own domain. memory:memory/2026-07-05.md:247:281.snippet: - When cron time hits → triggers flow directly (no HTTP call) with `trigger_type: cron` - Prevents duplicate runs (checks if successful run already happened for that cron slot) - Auto-advances to next cron slot after execution - Enable/disable individual schedules at runtime - Env vars: `SCHEDULER_ENABLED`, `SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS` (default 60000), `SCHEDULER_TIMEZONE` ### API endpoints added to router.ts - `GET /schedules` — list all scheduled flows (cron, next run, last run, enabled) - `GET /schedules/:name` — detail + recent run history - `POST /schedules/:name/enable` — re-enable disabled schedule - `POST /schedules/:name/disable` — pause schedule - `POST /schedules/:name/run` — manually
